programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In south Wales, beautician Rachael and her partner Nick take a risk by cooking dishes they've never made before, and Ameel shows off his powers of memory recall.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The teams create 24 classic Battenberg cakes and 24 reinvented ones, before taking on a Shakespearian-inspired theatrical scene. Whose chance to progress is to be, or not to be?

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in Oxford is resident ladies' man Marlon Williams, whose love island themed evening gets off to a rather shambolic start, and ends with smashing plates. His guests are party girl Heidi Kelloway, inventor Richard Berrecloth, housewife Victoria Collett and doggie day-care owner James Hearle.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day two in Oxford and inventor Richard Berrecloth is next to serve up an entirely homemade extravaganza, including the mysterious `Tournados Rossini' and some rather `unique' entertainment. For good-time girl Heidi and her flirting partner Marlon, food seems to be the last thing on their minds. Meanwhile, the wine is flowing a little too freely for 28-year-old former assistant funeral director Victoria Collett.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the turn of 28-year-old mum of three Victoria Collett, who starts day three in Oxford with a bad hangover, and a few culinary disasters. It's not long before the guests start grumbling and Richard starts criticising her cooking, calling her seafood starter simply `edible'. The dinner party perfectionist hopes to save the day with her Caribbean curry, but it leaves a bitter taste.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The knives are out on day four in Oxford as doggy day-care owner James Hearle attempts to fib his way through an interrogation of his cooking abilities. Tempers are rising between the guests and culinary-critique Richard Berrecloth, who has shocked the group with his brutal honesty. Meanwhile, James's four-legged friends threaten to turn the evening into a complete dog's dinner.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in Oxford, and party girl Heidi Kelloway is determined to lighten the mood and host the best dinner party of the week. Things turn sour when the other guests angrily confront cooking-critique and tactical-player Richard Berrecloth around the dinner table about his frank comments. Can Heidi scrape enough points from him to win the big cash prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in Glasgow is make-up artist Craig Alexander Wilson, who is throwing a glitz and glamour party, complete with edible glitter and sparklers. Craig's guests are trainee-dance teacher Mandy Wong, practical joker Paul Cowan, fun-loving Caroline Wardrope, and opinionated music teacher Siobhan Daly. However, Craig's attempt to guess his guests' ages doesn't go down very well.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Paul Cowan serves his guests Balmoral stack with whisky sauce on day two in Glasgow, as well as his mysterious cream puff. Is cheeky Paul more interested in dishing out practical jokes than food?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day three in Glasgow dance fanatic Mandy Wong hopes to bag herself the big cash prize by hosting a Latin dance fever evening with an Asian-inspired menu. But the night doesn't end so well as bubbly Caroline has to run to the bathroom during Mandy's dance routine, and Mandy serves up a disastrous dessert.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the turn of music teacher and professional flautist Siobhan Daley, who plans to beat the boys with a rock and pop icons evening on day four in Glasgow. The self-professed queen of cooking serves up cod and chorizo stew and shows off her piano skills for dessert.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in Glasgow and bubbly blonde Caroline Wardrope hosts a '70s-themed disco diva party with a mystery menu.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Blackpool, practising Buddhist Antonio and former redcoat Jason serve sushi, there's an eyebrow-raising discovery at fireman Daz and Kim's place, and foodies Martin and Kate serve Thai.

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Woodford Green, widower Shaz wants to preserve her husband's memories as well as future-proof her classic Victorian terraced home for her two growing kids.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Liv's dreamt of owning a holiday home on stunning Cyprus for years and is finally ready to make it happen. With a budget of £87k, can Ben Hillman make her dream come true?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Heather and John want a characterful bolthole on the beautiful island of Crete. Jasmine Harman shows them detached delights and an ice cream inspiration for £150k, but are any 'the one'?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Penny has been a therapist, artist and teacher for over 30 years, but now wants to move to the Spanish sunshine permanently and run her business from her new home in Málaga. Laura Hamilton lines up five great options for her budget of £120,000.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In Berkshire, Phil's with Eddie the Eagle and Karen, who've seen 100 houses in 18 months. Can they get gold this time? Kirstie helps Orla and Luke fend off Basingstoke's buyers.

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: In 2017, Leigh and Richard bought a derelict Cornish flour mill to restore and turn into their home. By 2021, rooms and landscaping were still unfinished. Kevin McCloud returns to see if this quirky old industrial building has finally become a home.

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.desc: The Night Coppers break up a mass brawl and tackle some urban explorers up a crane. And an inebriated nuisance neighbour doesn't want to go home and won't go to bed.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The celebs hit the sofa for a new series. What do Rylan and his mum, Tom Allen, Mo Gilligan, Vicky Pattison, Denise van Outen, and many others, think of this week's TV?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two 19-year-olds take their first steps into the dating world together. A hip-hop artist meets another musician. And can weightlifter Jack give bodybuilder Eloise a lift?

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Britain's busiest air ambulance teams return, bringing the hospital to the patient, from Lanchester to Ashdon, and a motorbike accident in the Peaks.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: A shocking hit and run leaves an elderly driver with serious head injuries, and a rude driver's outburst against police officers brings him under scrutiny which he could live to regret.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Cops spot a car speeding by the Tyne, and when they pull it over, a search uncovers a crack pipe. And PC Martain Anderson stakes out a suspected drug driver.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: At night, on the A1 in Newcastle, PCs Jan Simlesa and Marc Crammond spot a driver with his foot down, weaving in and out of lanes and bullying other motorists out of his way. In Sunderland, PCs are on the hunt for a BMW driver with no insurance.

programme.title: Black Horse Canyon
programme.desc: Frontier drama about two cowhands who try to capture a wild stallion responsible for luring the mares from a small town into the wild. The duo believe the stallion to be very valuable, especially if it is put to stud, and are keen to capture it as quickly as possible, but they soon encounter fierce competition from an unexpected quarter.

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel has an interview for her dream job at Ralph Lauren. Meanwhile, Monica competes to have a better relationship than Phoebe's, and Joey is arrested for stalking a sexy neighbour with whom Ross gets a date.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A woman's work-related injury claim unravels when footage shows her bungee jumping and jet skiing, while a scam to cash in on a mobile phone lost in a toilet goes down the drain.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Remake of the classic adventure tale of romance and revenge. A handsome sailor is unjustly accused of being a political traitor, betrayed by his best friend over a girl and imprisoned for 13 years in a rat-infested fortress. When he finally makes good his escape, the determined man plans to exact sweet revenge on his persecutors.

programme.desc: When a Hollywood producer decides to create a TV series about lifeguards, he wreaks havoc with the Baywatch crew.

programme.desc: A massive earthquake strands a diver, collapses C.J.'s apartment, traps Hobie in a garage and leaves Jackie's trailer dangling from a cliff.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch (David Hasselhoff) and his crew race against time to save Hobie (Jeremy Jackson), Jackie (Susan Anton) and others trapped by an earthquake.

programme.desc: Mitch learns the reason for Tracy's reluctance to take their relationship further; Logan and Caroline deal with two reckless youths on wave runners.

programme.desc: Mitch receives a mysterious package from his late father; Logan's strange reaction to a boat fire prompts Caroline to take him to a hypnotist.

Meanwhile, Danny has an intriguing mystery item to perplex fairgoers.

Sonali also drops in on a farm which specialises in growing a variety of sunflowers, many of which are taller than she is!

programme.desc: Charlie and Flo are in Egham to transform Neil and Rumen overgrown and unloved plot into an Islamic themed garden. They would love a sophisticated space that echoes some of their travels, particularly their trips to the middle east.

One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies.

programme.sub-title: St John's Wood United Synagogue, London

programme.title: Spirit Untamed
programme.desc: Lucky Prescott befriends a wild mustang named Spirit who shares her rebellious spirit. When a heartless wrangler plans to capture Spirit and his herd, Lucky and her new friends embark on a quest to rescue the horse that forever changed her life.

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Zog and Gadabout try to rescue Princess Pearl, who has been locked up in her father's castle. Based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Guiding the teams around an antiques centre in the Herefordshire town of Leominster are Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell. Meanwhile, Eric Knowles learns about the vibrant heritage of one of the town's oldest timber buildings.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on BBC television programmes.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Although originally from the UK, today's couple have been living in Canada since the 1980s. Now back home, and with Ginny Buckley's help, they are raring to find a new way of life and a new house in Dorset. Ginny also visits a farm helping to keep the elderly and retired gents fit in mind, body and soul.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Gulf Coast, where swamps and wetlands shelter black bears and alligators; manatees gather in freshwater springs, burrowing owls look for love in the suburbs and a rare ocelot raises her kittens in Texas' thornscrub.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Andes, Earth's longest mountain range; baby bears make treacherous descents, rare hummingbirds compete for mates, frogs freeze and revive, and lizards battle for territory -- all in a land of extremes, from glaciers to deserts.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC London News
programme.desc: News, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Wonders of the Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Joe Crowley, Margherita Taylor and Adam Henson explore a side of the Isle of Wight few visitors ever see. Beyond the beaches and bustling holiday towns, the island's rare geology and southerly location have shaped a landscape rich in surprises, from unusual marine life and distinctive habitats to crops more often associated with warmer climates.

programme.title: Tiger Island
programme.desc: In Nepal, a team of film-makers and big cat experts begin an expedition following two tiger mothers and their cubs, on an extraordinary island full of potential dangers.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A special edition of the roadshow celebrating the history of sport, featuring famous faces, moving stories and iconic items from some of the programme's most memorable sporting moments.

programme.title: Death Valley

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to see if the beautiful game still exists. He meets Brazil football icon Cafu, Scottish legend John McGinn, São Paulo ultras, US football fans and many others.

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: The story of Brian Clough's 44 day stint as manager of Leeds United Football Club. England, in 1974. When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revie's tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: On The Front Line
programme.sub-title: The Train Police: Tracking the Sex Offenders
programme.desc: Reports of sexual assault and harassment on trains and at stations are on the rise, but victims say CCTV cameras are often broken or of such poor quality that their attackers can't be identified. We follow British Transport Police officers and staff as they race against time to gather the crucial evidence they need to bring offenders to justice.

programme.sub-title: St John's Wood United Synagogue, London
programme.desc: Senior Rabbi Ivan Binstock welcomes us to an Orthodox Jewish morning prayer service, also known as Shacharit, at St John's Wood United Synagogue in north London.

programme.title: Spirit Untamed
programme.desc: Lucky Prescott befriends a wild mustang named Spirit who shares her rebellious spirit. When a heartless wrangler plans to capture Spirit and his herd, Lucky and her new friends embark on a quest to rescue the horse that forever changed her life.

programme.title: Zog and the Flying Doctors
programme.desc: Zog and Gadabout try to rescue Princess Pearl, who has been locked up in her father's castle. Based on the book by Julia Donaldson and Axel Scheffler.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Guiding the teams around an antiques centre in the Herefordshire town of Leominster are Kate Bliss and Philip Serrell. Meanwhile, Eric Knowles learns about the vibrant heritage of one of the town's oldest timber buildings.

programme.title: Points of View

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Although originally from the UK, today's couple have been living in Canada since the 1980s. Now back home, and with Ginny Buckley's help, they are raring to find a new way of life and a new house in Dorset. Ginny also visits a farm helping to keep the elderly and retired gents fit in mind, body and soul.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Gulf Coast, where swamps and wetlands shelter black bears and alligators; manatees gather in freshwater springs, burrowing owls look for love in the suburbs and a rare ocelot raises her kittens in Texas' thornscrub.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Andes, Earth's longest mountain range; baby bears make treacherous descents, rare hummingbirds compete for mates, frogs freeze and revive, and lizards battle for territory -- all in a land of extremes, from glaciers to deserts.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC Newsline

programme.sub-title: Wonders of the Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Joe Crowley, Margherita Taylor and Adam Henson explore a side of the Isle of Wight few visitors ever see. programme.title: Tiger Island
programme.desc: In Nepal, a team of film-makers and big cat experts begin an expedition following two tiger mothers and their cubs, on an extraordinary island full of potential dangers.

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: The story of Brian Clough's 44 day stint as manager of Leeds United Football Club. England, in 1974. Determined to impose his own style upon Revie's tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.desc: The Gulf Coast, where swamps and wetlands shelter black bears and alligators; manatees gather in freshwater springs, burrowing owls look for love in the suburbs and a rare ocelot raises her kittens in Texas' thornscrub.

programme.title: The Americas
programme.desc: The Andes, Earth's longest mountain range; baby bears make treacherous descents, rare hummingbirds compete for mates, frogs freeze and revive, and lizards battle for territory -- all in a land of extremes, from glaciers to deserts.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: 21st Century TV with Lucy Owen

programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC Wales Today
programme.desc: News and weather in Wales.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.sub-title: Wonders of the Isle of Wight
programme.desc: Joe Crowley, Margherita Taylor and Adam Henson explore a side of the Isle of Wight few visitors ever see. Beyond the beaches and bustling holiday towns, the island's rare geology and southerly location have shaped a landscape rich in surprises, from unusual marine life and distinctive habitats to crops more often associated with warmer climates.

programme.title: Tiger Island
programme.desc: In Nepal, a team of film-makers and big cat experts begin an expedition following two tiger mothers and their cubs, on an extraordinary island full of potential dangers.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A special edition of the roadshow celebrating the history of sport, featuring famous faces, moving stories and iconic items from some of the programme's most memorable sporting moments.

programme.title: Death Valley

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to see if the beautiful game still exists. He meets Brazil football icon Cafu, Scottish legend John McGinn, São Paulo ultras, US football fans and many others.

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: The story of Brian Clough's 44 day stint as manager of Leeds United Football Club. England, in 1974. When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revie's tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: On The Front Line
programme.sub-title: The Train Police: Tracking the Sex Offenders
programme.desc: Reports of sexual assault and harassment on trains and at stations are on the rise, but victims say CCTV cameras are often broken or of such poor quality that their attackers can't be identified. We follow British Transport Police officers and staff as they race against time to gather the crucial evidence they need to bring offenders to justice.

programme.desc: One morning in every city and town in the world, the human race wakes up to face the most surprising invasion yet. Everywhere, in every land, a forest has grown overnight and taken back the Earth.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda and Penny are forced into therapy for an assessment. How will they cope when trapped in a room together for a half hour session with an unnervingly silent therapist? All they have to do is sit quietly and control themselves. Testing times for any mother and daughter, let alone Miranda and Penny.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: It's Christmas and Miranda would like a relaxing and fun time without her parents. After Penny's Best Christmas Jumper Party, Miranda and Stevie try to spend Christmas at the shop with Gary, Clive and Tilly. However, after online present shopping doesn't arrive, an infuriating encounter at the post office, a run-in with some carol singers and a couple of arguments with friends, maybe Christmas would be better with her family.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The new year starts badly when Winnie's place is burgled and her mother's much-loved wedding ring is missing. Meanwhile, Cathy is worried about her impending birthday.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns get a visit from Uncle Micky, who is fresh out of prison with a new scam up his sleeve. Meanwhile, Winnie tries liven up her life with some daredevil stunts.

programme.title: The Nice Guys
programme.desc: A down-on-his-luck private eye works with a hired enforcer to investigate the disappearance of a young woman in 1977 Los Angeles.

programme.title: Bodies Bodies Bodies
programme.desc: When a group of 20-somethings gets stuck at a remote mansion during a hurricane, a party game gone very, very wrong ends with a dead body on the ground and fake friends at every turn as they try to find the killer among them.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Donna and Janet are furious with Gaz and demand he decides who he wants to be with by three o'clock. He has no idea who to choose and decides to audition them both. Elsewhere, Louise is worried her new boy-band boyfriend Scott is going to run off on tour with a groupie and turns all Fatal Attraction on him, before Gaz gives her advice on how to make a man fall in love with you.

programme.desc: Yolande helps Denise as she processes life-changing news, and Ian is blissfully unaware that his happy life is about to change.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian's world comes crashing down. Max shocks Cindy.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max's emotions force him into a reckless decision, Yolande covers for Denise as she's pulled in two directions, and George makes a worrying discovery.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: George makes a decision for the sake of his family. The sun rises on Walford as the 'night that changes everything' comes to a close, but it's clear the tricky road ahead has only just begun for some.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Five years ago, a schoolgirl was murdered by her boyfriend. It seemed like case closed, but not according to Pip Fitz-Amobi. She decides to uncover the truth and find the real killer.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: Five years ago, a schoolgirl was murdered by her boyfriend. It seemed like case closed, but not according to Pip Fitz-Amobi. She decides to uncover the truth and find the real killer.

programme.title: Everest
programme.desc: Climbers from two expeditions fight for survival during a fierce blizzard on Mount Everest.

programme.title: Daisy Maskell: Insomnia and Me
programme.desc: Daisy Maskell, the UK's youngest ever breakfast radio host, has suffered from insomnia since childhood. For as long as she can remember, she has survived on as little as a couple of hours of sleep each night and is wide awake until the early hours of the morning. With an ever-increasing workload, running on empty is starting to take its toll, so Daisy wants to uncover the roots of her insomnia and take the first steps towards treatment. She is not alone.

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings navigate life with Feelgood whilst dodging Leo's suspicions. Jamie takes Tiana on an impromptu date, while Ms Jenkins grows worried about Tanika's home life.

programme.title: Just Act Normal
programme.desc: The siblings hire a fake Jackie to fool Ms Jenkins. Tiana juggles work, studies and family, clashing with Shanice. Feelgood tries to leave drug dealing behind.

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: It is Dad's 65th birthday, and Mum has organised a surprise party. Dane's aunties and uncles are all asking the questions he doesn't want to answer, about his worklife and lovelife, while comparing his progress with that of his perfect cousin Christian. Dane's lifelong best friend Stefan and girlfriend Nicola both try to help Dane keep his cool.

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: With Dane now finding himself engaged, the pressure is on for him to leave the parental home and start a new life with Nicola. Dane will need to save a fortune if he's ever going to find a habitable place to rent, yet his office life is getting worse and worse, thanks to his new boss, the ferocious, flirtatious Kerry.

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Having made Dane suffer, Nicola agrees to carry on with their engagement. Plans are made for a wedding meeting, where Dane's parents will be introduced to Jamaar, Nicola's Dad. Jamaar is a part time/failed DJ and a part time/failed Dad, who's only been back in touch with Nicola since she's found financial security.

programme.title: Sunny D
programme.desc: Dane's volatile behaviour means he's a disgrace with both his family, and with his now ex-girlfriend Nicola. His best friend Stefan attempts to pep talk him through love and life, reminding him just how funny he is, and why they're such close friends, but Dane is on a downward slide.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: A young man, Sam Gilula, arrives at Frank and Marie's and everyone is surprised when he and Frank fondly embrace. Sam explains Frank had been quite an inspiration to him as a lad, and Ray and Robert make fun of Frank, wondering how he could have inspired anyone.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Robert get into tricky territory over who will have responsibility for Marie in the future.

programme.sub-title: The Show Where Woody Shows Up

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles prepares for a Valentine's date at Frasier's apartment, until a crease in his trousers escalates into a series of calamities. programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier has Niles hire a legal shark to help with his divorce, but fears having to testify.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart's new job and a dramatic roof-top protest at Krustyburger inspire Lisa to become an eco-radical, especially when she sees how cute one of the protestors is. programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When trying to deal with a rogue badger, Homer discovers that the area code for the Simpsons' half of Springfield (the poor half) has changed. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray makes the mistake of asking Debra why she married him, rekindling memories of his proposal and his family's involvement, and bringing back Ray's fears that Debra felt pressured into saying yes.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is afraid that Debra is marrying him for the wrong reason.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray moves his family into his parents' house when the termite exterminators visit, and Debra takes the opportunity to turn the tables by interfering in Frank and Marie's life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Frank backs into Robert's police car, Debra decides that he's not a safe driver and that the kids can't travel in his car.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra hires a babysitter but becomes jealous of her popularity with the kids, and her plans to remedy the situation end in disaster.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Inappropriate Sister
programme.desc: After returning from another disappointing audition, Joey tries to write a play in which he can star. Meanwhile, Rachel gets a date with her neighbour Danny, but is perturbed to discover his extremely intimate relationship with his sexy sister.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Resolutions
programme.desc: The friends make their New Year resolutions, and Joey tries to arrange for Chandler to kiss Monica at midnight at a New Year's Eve party.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Chandler's Work Laugh
programme.desc: Monica is annoyed when she discovers Chandler is ingratiating himself with his new boss Doug. He even has a fake laugh which he uses whenever Doug cracks a joke. Meanwhile, Rachel is obsessed with finding out the gossip between Monica and Chandler after overhearing a sexy conversation between them, and Ross astounds everyone with his new girlfriend, a blast from the past.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: At her grandmother's funeral, Phoebe is astounded to meet her biological father, Frank Sr, who she has been trying to find for years. Elsewhere, Chandler tells perfectionist Monica that she is useless at massage, and Joey's fondness for an expensive bag loses him an audition.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Everybody Finds Out
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica's relationship becomes less of a secret, and Ross must prove to his boss that he's sane in order to return to work.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Girl Who Hits Joey
programme.desc: Chandler becomes anxious when he thinks Monica wants more from their relationship, and Ross moves into his new apartment and fails to impress his new neighbours. Meanwhile, Joey is afraid to end his relationship with a diminutive woman who punches him hard every time she thinks something is fun.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe finds a police officer's badge and goes on a spree of arrests until she meets up with the owner, while Joey wonders if he is in love with Monica.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel has an interview for her dream job at Ralph Lauren. All goes well until she inadvertently kisses the interviewer, and at the second interview she mistakenly berates him for his `sexual intentions'. Meanwhile, Monica competes to have a better relationship than Phoebe's, and Joey is arrested for stalking a sexy neighbour with whom Ross gets a date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel's new boss and colleague both smoke. When Rachel discovers that they make business decisions without her in the smoking room, she pretends to be a serious smoker so she can be part of the discussion. Meanwhile, Ross's son and Joey audition for the same commercial, but only one of them can get the part.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Can't Flirt
programme.desc: Ross really likes the pretty pizza-delivery woman, but his flirting technique is awful. Meanwhile, a nasty surprise awaits Joey when he tells everyone about his big television appearance.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: The gang are invited to a wedding of a mutual friend. Nick is worried that he'll run into Caroline, his ex, and so asks Jess to be his pretend girlfriend. Meanwhile, Schmidt is on the prowl for single women but ends up with his sexual arch-enemy and Winston relishes his role as usher by getting competitive on the dance-floor.

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Jess accidentally walks in on a naked Nick and ruins his self-confidence before a date with a co-worker. Meanwhile, Winston busies himself on Wikipedia.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Free Porn

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Wedding Dresses

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Ross reminisce separately about when they dated each other, and the friends get invitations to Ross's wedding, but not all of them are going.

programme.title: The Angry Birds Movie
programme.desc: Flightless birds lead a mostly happy existence, except for Red, who just can't get past the daily annoyances of life. His temperament leads him to anger management class, where he meets fellow misfits Chuck and Bomb. Red becomes even more agitated when his feathered brethren welcome green pigs to their island paradise. As the swine begin to get under his skin, Red joins forces with Chuck and Bomb to investigate the real reason behind their mysterious arrival.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes' Latin America road trip continues - they are now in Costa Rica, where Brad's fear of heights is tested as Barney's arranged a breathtaking way to take in the coastal views in what can only be described as a tiny roofless helicopter. With Costa Rica known for its diverse wildlife, the pair stop off to meet a quirky animal lover and then head to the capital city of San Jose, where they get their skates on as they join a local roller derby team for a match against their biggest rivals.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad

programme.desc: As Mark's best man, Jeremy has organised a stag weekend consisting of Mark and Jeremy on a canal boat on the Shropshire Union Canal. Unfortunately, it's cold, there's nothing to do, and Jeremy is instantly bored, but things soon spice up when Jeremy meets a new girl, then does something unspeakable to her pet dog. Later, Mark gets offered an exciting new job in India, possibly being his way out of marriage to Sophie.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Beverly learns that Barry lied about joining the wrestling team, she takes matters into her own hands. Erica flakes on a promise to take Adam to the premiere of `Return of the Jedi'.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Murray's high school free throw record is broken, he attends a banquet as the guest of honour. Meanwhile, Barry decides to throw a party.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Rachel and Adam look back on their unforgettable 1980s Aussie soap wedding. Does Rachel appreciate the effort Adam went to especially for her?

programme.title: LEGO Masters: Australia vs The World
programme.desc: In a colossal challenge, the teams design minifigs interacting with life-size household items, from a kitchen calamity to toilet troubles!

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister that features a variety of cats doing strange things, some soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope.

programme.title: The Amazing Race Australia: Celebrity Edition
programme.desc: It's the grand finale! The top three teams are pushed to their limits in Kuala Lumpur, before a pit stop finish like never before... Who will be the winners?

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With a fractious atmosphere in the air, the couples head off for a weekend retreat. And there are big questions looming for all the newly-weds.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: As the retreat winds down, one couple make amends while others begin planning for the future. But for one pair, a serious roadblock arises.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: With Decision Day looming, the couples prepare for the biggest call of their lives. Meanwhile, one bride is blindsided by life-changing news.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
programme.desc: Sonic tries to navigate the complexities of life on Earth with his newfound best friend, a human named Tom Wachowski. They must soon join forces to prevent the evil Dr Robotnik from capturing Sonic and using his powers for world domination.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge puts a hurtful word in Lisa's brain; Bart gets in shpae.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Portrait of a Lackey on Fire
programme.desc: Smithers finds true love with a famous fashion designer, but his new relationship may destroy Springfield.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Teenage Homer and Grampa take a road trip to look for his mum.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Fat Tony becomes Godfather to Maggie Simpson.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Mr. Burns fight over a brash young football prodigy.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer takes on Mr Burns in a labour struggle that rapidly spirals out of control. When Homer leads the workers out on strike, Mr Burns retaliates by hiring robots to replace them and shutting off power to the city.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: So It Has Come to This: The Simpsons Clip Show
programme.desc: Bart's special April Fools' Day prank seriously misfires when the whole house explodes. Homer ends up in the hospital where a candy machine falls on top of him, sending him into a coma. While clips are shown of past blows to his head, family and visitors debate what to do about Homer.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: At his high-school reunion, Homer wins several awards including `Most Weight Gained' and `Most Improved Odour', but the Principal takes them away when he finds out that Homer never graduated. Homer vows to graduate and win them back by going to night school.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Springfield holiday of Whacking Day is coming, when live snakes get whacked with sticks, and Homer can hardly wait, but Lisa is horrified and persuades Barry White to help by attracting the snakes to safety.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Krusty the Klown's network show gets cancelled, Bart and Lisa unite to get him back on TV by enlisting some of Krusty's celebrity friends to appear on a comeback special. On the guest list are Bette Midler, Hugh Hefner, the Red Hot Chili Peppers, Elizabeth Taylor, Johnny Carson and Luke Perry. After the show the celebrities head for Moe's to hang out together.

programme.title: Moonfall
programme.desc: The world stands on the brink of annihilation when a mysterious force knocks the moon from its orbit and sends it hurtling toward Earth. With only weeks before impact, three unlikely heroes team up for a dangerous mission into space to save humanity.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britain's best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers gets stuck into a new series and more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Emily, Miss Essex 2016/17, is looking for a man who won't just see her as a trophy girlfriend. Ketan, who has autism, meets Brodie, who's looking for a man with quirks.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: First Dates restaurant puts out the bunting and welcomes a new congregation of daters, all of whom want to find a love like Meghan and Prince Harry's. Among them is history student Abbie, who's 20, and is a descendant of Henry VIII.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The Goggleboxers gets stuck into a new series and more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Pantaleone's in Denver, Colorado, where he meets the Italian restaurant's owner Pete, who is truly stuck in the past. Pete stubbornly maintains that he makes the `Best Pizza in Denver', based on a review from the 1980s.

programme.title: Significant Other
programme.desc: A young couple take a remote backpacking trip through the Pacific Northwest and face sinister events.

programme.title: D-Day, the Sixth of June
programme.desc: It is the night before D-Day. One ship, carrying Special Force Six, leaves ahead of the main invasion on a dangerous mission. On board are British Colonel Wynter and American Captain Parker, who each, in flashback, reminisce about their separate involvement with beauteous Valerie Russell.

programme.title: The Longest Day
programme.desc: The heroics and tragedies of the Allied invasion of Normandy, from the tense wait for the weather to break before the huge armada could set sail, to the bloody battles fought on the beaches of France.

programme.title: Spies in Disguise
programme.desc: A charming super spy and his geeky scientist companion are almost exact opposites. But what the scientist lacks in social skills he makes up for in invention, creating awesome gadgets for the spy to use on his epic missions.

programme.title: Sister Act
programme.desc: A cabaret singer witnesses a murder and goes undercover as a nun. In the convent, she is put in charge of coaching the choir, which she transforms into a toe-tapping rhythm group. As a result, the church's congregation swells until even the Pope comes to witness pop classics such as `My Guy' performed to glorify the Lord, but of course, her fame gives away her hiding place to the killer.

programme.title: Django Unchained Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Quentin Tarantino and some of his cool cast talk about their exciting new western adventure, `Django Unchained'.

programme.title: Django Unchained
programme.desc: A former slave joins forces with the German bounty hunter who freed him and helps hunt down the South's most-wanted criminals, all in the hope of finding his long-lost wife.

programme.title: The Entity
programme.desc: Parapsychologists investigate a woman's claims that an invisible being has been molesting her. Based on a true story.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits an old nightclub that makes freshly smoked salmon and a New York restaurant that sells cheeseburger soup.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy rolls out for medium-rare done right.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Sauteed rib-eye with french fries; beef heart; fresh bread; schnitzel.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy finds a farm restaurant in New York, a fishing shop turned eatery in California, and a Mexican joint with tuna tacos in Ohio.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree shares the good stuff with the chocolate lovers in her life, starting with a triple chocolate tiramisu for her best friend. Then, with her son's help, she makes crispy chocolate dippers to use in a crazy chocolate shake.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond has money-saving ideas for transforming random cuts of beef into four delicious dishes, including slow cooker Sunday stew, teriyaki beef stir-fry, meat pies and grilled beef salad.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes four of her favourite recipes lighter and healthier in 16 minutes. Try her lighter chicken parmesan, Asian noodle salad, taco skillet and lighter fried pork chops with white beans and spinach.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree spends time with her son, Bryce, making beef fajita nachos. The next day, she prepares slow cooker white chicken chili for dinner and orange-vanilla monkey bread as a breakfast treat, while Bryce cooks meat and potato scramble.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond welcomes a new cowboy to the ranch with a frontier-style dinner kit of his favourite food, including short rib sandwiches, roasted butternut squash salad and salted caramel frosted brownies.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's comfort from start to finish and Ree's spelling it out in an old-fashioned, feel-good way. She cooks up tuna noodle casserole, meatballs in sweet and sour sauce, glazed apple dumplings and Welsh rarebit.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes a frugal but amazing paella with chicken thighs, spicy chorizo and prawns from the freezer. There's also crisp Cajun fishcakes with a classic chopped salad and Jamie's snake in the hole.

programme.title: Jamie's Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: Jamie makes a thrifty curry with chicken drumsticks and frugal but posh beans on toast.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by Galton Blackiston, his pal and a top-class, Michelin-starred chef. They don diving suits to swim with seals, and hire an unusual mode of transport to take them around the islands. Galton visits a sea salt farm and delivers some back to James, who uses it to cook up some wonderful salt-baked sea bass. James finds an unusual restaurant situated in the middle of a post office, and finishes off the day cooking roast duck by a stunning seashore.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James returns to one of his favourite places in the UK: the beautiful county of Cornwall. Joining him is his close pal and Michelin-starred chef Galton Blackiston. During their trip they discover some world-class Cornish ice cream, and Galton visits Newlyn fish market to pick up some Cornish cod. James cooks the cod along with Cornish oysters and a warm tartare sauce. They both round off the day with a painting challenge on Sennen beach, where they try out their oil painting skills.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel Allen uses her kitchen cunning to make delicious meals. Here, she uses local ingredients including Dublin Bay prawns and Irish potatoes.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel and family re-invent a timeless classic, Roast Chicken and a wonderful Summery salad.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Simply delicious food for hot sunny weather is the order of the day as Rachel whips up a Nutty Quinoa Salad, a Potato, Smoked Salmon & Dill Tart and a Spinach, Bacon and Gruyère frittata.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: A slow recipe using lamb.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Kids: Food that the children will love and that Mum and Dad will most likely tuck into as well! Sun-Blushed Tomato or Parma Cam, Goats Cheese & Red Onion Pizza, Cheesy Chorizo Dough balls and Cookies & Cream Cheesecake are all rustled up in Rachel's kitchen, plus there are tracks of the trade from the cookery school and a glimpse of family life as Rachel and her daughter pick their own veg.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel has some fantastic food ideas for home workers, including roasted butternut squash salad, and red lentil soup with cumin and coriander.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Irish chef Rachel explores the joy of cooking with home grown ingredients, with dishes including duck plum spring rolls with sesame pak choi.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Sample a typical working day for Rachel and discover foods which provide fuel for busy times, including smoked salmon and lemon risotto with asparagus.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel makes some favourites designed to get everyone round the table, including cheesy meatballs with spaghetti, and butterscotch apple pudding.

programme.title: Rachel Allen's Everyday Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel's busy working life is again the inspiration, as she explores alternative takes on a packed lunch, and food that works on the table or on the go.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue welcomes award-winning chef Tommy Banks as she makes two-tone soups and is joined in the kitchen by her husband John for a goat curd and tomato galette.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's going for amped-up pork tacos in Sacramento, CA, and a BBQ joint in Palm Beach Gardens, FL, is bringing brisket to a stacked sloppy joe. Plus, a real-deal Cambodian spot in San Jose, CA, is serving primo pork belly noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: From Bunny Chow to Chicken Oysters
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's digging into the unexpected! A South African spot in Black Mountain, NC, is cooking lights-out lamb curry, a chef in San Jose, CA, is bringing big flavour to beef barbacoa, and a Boynton Beach, FL, joint is serving up chicken oysters.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Tennessee Guy visits a spot that smokes the whole hog, a sausage cafe in Kentucky, and he finds stuffed pork chops in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In California Guy visits a diner for meatloaf, a chilli spot in Cincinnati, and an Irish pub in Florida for a Guinness beef stew.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a farmer's market in South Carolina, an Italian spot in New York, and finds a fresh spin on a fish classic in Honolulu.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits an old nightclub that makes freshly smoked salmon and a New York restaurant that sells cheeseburger soup.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: On this trip, Guy's tasting it all. In Chicago, Illinois, a dive is refining small plate Mexican food with organic ingredients like shiitake mushroom empanadas and flautas with home-made queso fresco.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy rolls out for medium-rare done right.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Sauteed rib-eye with french fries; beef heart; fresh bread; schnitzel.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's going for amped-up pork tacos in Sacramento, CA, and a BBQ joint in Palm Beach Gardens, FL, is bringing brisket to a stacked sloppy joe. Plus, a real-deal Cambodian spot in San Jose, CA, is serving primo pork belly noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Viewer favorites.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy finds fresh crab in San Francisco, homemade granola in Nevada, and boiled turkey in North Carolina.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam heads to Chicago, where a plate of overstuffed sandwiches with fries is awaiting him.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Atlanta is the next stop on Adams culinary tour, where he attempts to finish a 13-pound Carnivore Pizza. However, this is one challenge he'll need some help with.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam takes on the hottest curry in the country at New York's Brick Lane Curry House.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam heads to Portland, Oregon where a habanero fritter challenge await him.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In St Louis, Adam attempts to down almost a gallon of the best milkshake in town.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam faces a hot task as he tries to consume a plate of Atomic Hot Wings in Pittsburgh.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: The next stop on Adam Richman's culinary tour of the United States is Boston, where he encounters the giant Eagle's Challenge Burger. Does it come with a giant soda?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam Richman heads to New Orleans, where he takes on a plate of 15 dozen oysters at Acme's Oyster House. Did he remember to bring a knife?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam tackles a five pound omelette made with 12 eggs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: How will Adam fare when he faces a spicy ramen made with ten different chilies?

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: On the next leg of his UK tour, James is joined in glorious Devon by his friend and Michelin-starred chef Simon Hulstone. James visits an extraordinary mushroom farm situated in a bungalow, and makes a tasty steak and mushroom dish. Simon samples some award-winning cheese, which inspires James to make a cheese platter fit for a king. The day ends with a trip to the Art Deco splendour of Burgh Island.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by pal and Michelin-starred chef Mark Jordan, and together they set out to explore the delights of Jersey aboard mini motor bikes. James samples the delights of one of Jersey's most famous exports, the Jersey Royal potato, and he uses them to cook up a squid risotto with a difference. Mark Jordan gets a lesson in oyster shucking, and James discovers an island speciality: Jersey black butter, which he uses to create a delicious sweet treat.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: This time the judges set challenges inspired by the roaring twenties, including a slapstick Signature and a tiered Showstopper offering a twist on their favourite tipple.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The cake layers stack up in an elegant and challenging bake for Dessert Week. There's also a spherical celebration cake and a Technical where success lies in precise decoration.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Festival Week as the bakers draw inspiration from around the world, including something deep fried and Italian, and a spectacular Indonesian celebration cake.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers have their knowledge of pastry tested to the max with a savoury take on a French classic, a multi-layered Moroccan Technical, and a towering Showstopper.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: James' adventure begins in San Sebastian, a city boasting 16 Michelin stars - he experiences pintxos, tries whole-grilled turbot, samples local cider and shops at the fish market while also cooking hake, lamb, Basque stew and pintxos.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 12 of San Jose's hottest hot wings without taking a drink - for five minutes after the food is gone.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Denver, Adam tackles a giant breakfast burrito containing seven potatoes, a pound of ham and 12 eggs.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's British adventure travels as far to the east of England as this journey will go, to Norfolk, where he is joined by his good old mate and top local chef, Galton Blackiston. They meet in Cromer, where Galton has a successful fish and chip shop, which inspires James to cook pan-fried cod with a warm tartar sauce on the beach. Then James heads to Norfolk Lavender to find out why it grows so well in this county and how it's distilled for oil.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: No adventure around the UK would be complete without a trip to the capital city, London. James is checking out some of his old haunts and stopping in on Clare Smyth's brand new restaurant Core for a bite to eat and a catch up with the world's best female chef. Then high up on the rooftops of London, he cooks up spiced pigeon breast with dhal. Next, James ventures into south east London to a knife forgery in Peckham. He gets hands on in the process of making Japanese style knives.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's going for amped-up pork tacos in Sacramento, CA, and a BBQ joint in Palm Beach Gardens, FL, is bringing brisket to a stacked sloppy joe. Plus, a real-deal Cambodian spot in San Jose, CA, is serving primo pork belly noodles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Handmade patties with fried green tomatoes; homemade bread; scrapple.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Hawaii Guy goes to a water side spot with a real catch of the day, a hot dog place with lobster dogs and a luau with roasted pig.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's dessert day and with just one place remaining at the banquet feast, each chef is giving everything they've got for a chance to say thank you to the heroic NHS staff. All the chefs have significantly re-worked or re-invented their desserts since the regional heats, proving that they'll do everything they can in a bid for that final winning spot.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the grand finale as the winning chefs cook their dishes at a banquet for the heroes of the NHS. Having arrived at St Bartholomew's Hospital, the chefs are shown the incredible location for event, the almost three hundred year old Great Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Some of the nation's greatest chefs battle for the chance to cook a glittering Olympic banquet. The feast will honour the hopes and achievements of the country's sporting heroes, and it will be a glorious celebration of Britain's culinary talents. This time it is the battle of the Scottish chefs - returning contender Alan Murchison and newcomers Colin Buchan and Mark Greenaway.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contender Alan Murchison and newcomers Colin Buchan and Mark Greenaway must cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be grilled mackerel with beetroot meringues and horseradish ice cream, crab `cannelloni' or poached halibut with squid ink? Great British Menu veteran Jeremy Lee will decide who goes through to the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Sir Matthew Pinsent takes time out to meet one of the chefs and give them valuable tips on competing at the highest level. All three chefs are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. This is the big one, the main course, but which one will get top marks? Will it be barbecued veal with macaroni cheese, spinach and watercress; beef rump and terrine; or loin of blackface lamb with sweetbreads?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs - returning contender Alan Murchison and newcomers Colin Buchan and Mark Greenaway. All of them are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. This is the final chance to impress `Great British Menu' veteran Jeremy Lee with their dessert dishes - `Going for Gold', modern day Eton mess, and beetroot cake with chocolate mousse and fennel ice cream.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from Scotland. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. Only one chef can make it through to the national finals to represent Scotland. If they win, one of their dishes could be on the menu at The Olympic Feast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Central chefs Aktar Islam and Daniel Clifford and newcomer Paul Foster kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be pork neck with ribwort plantain and pork scratchings, veal tartare with caramelised sweetbread and burnt onions or breast of squab with confit leg and chilli and sweetcorn shoots?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Aktar Islam, Daniel Clifford and Paul Foster cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be poached ray wing with chicken skin and sea vegetables, stuffed red mullet with roasted artichoke and parmesan puree, or pickled mackerel with smoked langoustine and infused coconut milk?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs Aktar Islam, Daniel Clifford and Paul Foster cook the big one, the main course, but which one will get top marks? Will it be Goosnargh duck breast and hearts with potatoes cooked in pine, slow poached chicken and sweetcorn egg or coriander crumbed lamb loin with sweetbread bhajis and pea pulao?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the final chance for chefs Aktar Islam, Daniel Clifford and Paul Foster to impress Glynn Purnell as they make their desserts - whipped sea buckthorn with meringue and crispy rice, raspberry and tarragon roulade with cookie dough and tarragon oil, and dark chocolate with raspberry jelly and raspberry sorbet. Plus, paralympian Daniel Greaves offers his advice to one chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the Central region. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the judges - Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges are not easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. Only one chef can make it through to the national finals to represent the Central region at a glittering Olympic Banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In this battle of the North East chefs, returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin prepare their respective starters. Which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Stephanie's goat's cheese, Charlie's wild rabbit with carrot and Douglas fir needles, or Colin's quail in the woods?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin cook their fish course here. Which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Stephanie's `see food', Charlie's beetroot marinated monkfish with cauliflower and sea radish, or Colin's mullet on the sea bed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin cook their main course here. Which one will take gold? Will it be Stephanie's `team spirit re-vealed', Charlie's Dexter beef, Littlebourne snails, marrow bone and wild garlic, or Colin's hay-smoked pig's head with mock apple and textures of onion?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contender Stephanie Moon and newcomers Colin McGurran and Charlie Lakin have a chance to impress with their dessert dishes. Will it be Stephanie's `podium of puddings', Charlie's Earl Grey and strawberry souffle with gorse flower ice cream, or Colin's chocolate, rhubarb and custard?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the North East region. One chef left the competition on the previous day and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do. Who will make it through to the to represent the North East region in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In the battle of the Northern Ireland chefs - returning contenders Chris Fearon, Chris Bell and Niall McKenna begin with their starters. Which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Chris Bell with tea and biscuits, rabbit, black pudding and rhubarb salad; Niall with pig sweetbreads, black truffle and baked onion; or Chris Fearon with clay pigeon shoot?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Chris Fearon, Chris Bell and Niall McKenna cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be Chris Bell's red wine poached turbot and bourguignon of snails; Niall's turbot with asparagus, blood orange sauce and rose powder; or Chris Fearon's skate rings?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Chris Fearon, Chris Bell and Niall McKenna cook their main course here, but which one will take gold? Will it be Chris Bell's roast lissara duck, bakewell tart, cocoa and basil; Niall's dexter beef with miso and fresh almonds; or Chris Fearon's spring jump lamb?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Chris Fearon, Chris Bell and Niall McKenna present their dessert dishes. Will it be Chris Bell's summer salad of jasmine panna cotta, apricots and pistachio cake; Niall's apple sorbet, elderflower custard and whiskey jelly; or Chris Fearon's the torch? Plus, Olympian Amir Khan gives valuable tips on competing at the highest level.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the Northern Ireland region. With two chef's remaining, the must cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do. Just one chef can make it through to the National Finals to represent Northern Ireland.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan kick things off with their starters. Johnnie makes foie gras ice cream, cherry ravioli and iberico ham; Aiden offers black cherry and foie gras terrine with palm sugar mousse; and Simon's entry is grilled salad, truffle custard, cheese foam and cobnut crisp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective fish courses. Johnnie concocts a Recreation of the Sea; Aiden makes beetroot poached salmon, caviar, razor clam, citrus and fennel salad; and Simon serves up lobster with pickled beetroot, sweet apple and cuckoo flower.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan are challenged to cook their mains and Marcus Wareing judges the dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective desserts. It's the final chance for the chefs to impress veteran Marcus Wareing as he decides who will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs compete for the chance to cook at a glittering Olympic banquet. The North West finalists go head-to-head as they prepare a lavish meal for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In the battle of the London and South East chefs newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their starter menus. Will it be Phil with spring salad with goat's milk puree, pickled asparagus and quail's eggs; Graham with pickled rabbit, wild garlic quinoa and frozen rabbit liver parfait; or Marcus with iced lamb's liver parfait, malt loaf, fingerling limes and rose geranium?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their fish courses. Will it be Phil with a tasting of Cornish mackerel with oysters, mussels, winkles and samphire; Marcus with pollock, peas, coconut and elderflower; or Graham with octopus, blood orange, feta cheese, squid ink tuile and black olive salt?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness cook their main courses, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Phil with roast loin of lamb with pie and mash, carrots, nettles and mint; Graham with slow cooked marsh lamb breast, rasher and spiced rissole; or Marcus with blade of beef cooked in hay, tendons and beetroot?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their dessert dishes here. Will it be Phil with rhubarb and custard souffle, Marcus with asparagus, goat's curd and black olive, or Graham with lemon meringue? Veteran Jason Atherton decides who goes through to the judges and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from the London and South East region. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. The chef's task is to create a menu which captures the Olympic spirit.

programme.desc: Examining the link between geomagnetic anomalies and Somerset County's paranormal wonders. A look at strange spectral sightings, and learning about a notable resident who could be the source of the hauntings.

programme.title: Haunted Case Files
