programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Adyan has never had a garden before, but now she is married to Araam, she has her hands on one for the very first time. Unfortunately, its main features are a long strip of concrete leading to nowhere and a six-inch-high barbecue! Charlie and Lee go head-to-head to win the couple over. Getting the concrete strip out is the first challenge for the team, but once that's done, the winning plan comes to life. The team add a pergola that incorporates a swing, a barbecue area for Araam.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chloe and Peter are a young couple who have just moved to Godalming in Surrey with their dog. However, their new garden is a neglected, overgrown mess that has a strict tree preservation order on four of the trees. The couple envision a blend of arts and crafts and French formality in their new garden - how will the designers, Charlie and Chris, interpret these very different, almost conflicting ideas in their pitches? With a budget of £6,000.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh visits a charming seaside garden in East Wittering, Sussex, he shows how to make a nautical obelisk and demonstrates how to adapt your garden to cope with damaging coastal winds, while Laetitia Maklouf sprinkles some seaside chic on a tiny balcony and Charlotte Uhlenbroek shares the secrets of a coastal wildlife garden, plus Alan's tips on how to capture the essence of the sea to create your own mini seaside escape, even if you live in the middle of a city.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan demonstrates how to build a rose garden, and visits one in Wendover, Buckinghamshire. Plus, Laetitia Maklouf reveals ways to extend the life of a rose into winter by making rosewater, Valentine Warner shows how roses can even be the key ingredient in a spicy sauce, and Alan suggests stunning ways to display the flower - from rustic pergolas to pavilions from paradise.

programme.title: Yorkshire By The Sea
programme.desc: Meet the locals striving and thriving to earn an honest crust on Yorkshire's coast. Fishing family the Robsons head to Whitby to repair their boat, and we take a dip in Scarborough's South Bay with a group of sea swimmers for a full-moon sea swim.

programme.title: Yorkshire By The Sea
programme.desc: Whitby distillers Jess and Luke create a tasty tipple from foraged seaside ingredients. And in Scarborough, pioneering sea-farmer Wave checks out his seaweed harvest of sugar kelp.

programme.title: Yorkshire By The Sea
programme.desc: In Scarborough, we hit the waves with an accessible surfing group, and in Robin Hood's Bay, we follow the ambitious couple launching their first wine from their vineyard.

programme.desc: Ronnie Barker stars as General Futtock, who has a motley group of guests stay at his country estate - and chaos ensues. Contains 70s social attitudes.

programme.desc: James "Biggles" Bigglesworth and 1980s Jim Ferguson discover they have the ability to travel through time to each other's eras. Determined to stop the Germans changing the outcome of World War One, the duo attempt to destroy a German super weapon.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for previously unseen material, and a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: It's a high-stakes gallery break-in as the teams face cryptic clues, rat-infested air ducts and nerve-jangling decisions to pull off the ultimate job: stealing the Mona Lisa.

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: The teams are trapped in a brain-bending, off-the-scale world where nothing quite measures up, and they must locate and detonate a teeny-tiny bomb before time runs out!

programme.title: The Way Out
programme.desc: It's a fiery finale as the teams race to rescue the hapless Donna from a burning flat. With a blaze to put out and puzzles to ponder, they must keep calm as the heat rises.

programme.title: Clash of the Comics
programme.desc: Professional wrestling action - featuring top comedians - that includes Ed Gamble versus Phil Wang and Maisie Adam up against multi-time champion wrestler Nina Samuels.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee's feckless dad, Frank, announces that he's engaged to be married, Lee cannot believe that any woman would want to marry him. There must be something wrong with her. Lucy invites Frank and Carol to dinner to allay Lee's fears. Carol turns out to be perfect, but now Lee is even more nervous. Frank seems to dote on her. If Carol dumps Frank, Frank will be devastated.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee joins Facebook for the first time and is contacted by an ex-girlfriend from 20 years ago, Lucy is annoyed that she cares so much. Her feelings are complicated by the sudden reappearance of a friend from university, with whom she had a brief relationship. Threatened by fond memories from the past, Lee and Lucy start behaving badly.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: The family visit to the war graves in Normandy gets off to a bad start, and Lucy discovers that her grandfather, Wendy's father, was not the man she thought he was.

programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc is host, with panellists writer and director Armando Iannucci and journalist Anushka Asthana joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life is Goodish
programme.desc: Dave Gorman performs a series of stand-up jokes about the digital footprints people leave behind when surfing the internet.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: In retaliation to Ron's `guys-only' wilderness group, Leslie takes the women on an adventure weekend. Ben is forced into joining Donna and Tom for a day of relaxation and Chris spends time with Jerry.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Nat can't cope with both voodoo magic and Heavy D. Luckily, Sean has an emergency vehicle to hand!

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Babbling Sean Kelly is the auction host with the most on the south coast, where an uplifting prize gets the offers rolling. John gives Nat flak for blind bidding.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team look at track cars, and put Vauxhall's VX220 Turbo through its paces. As the search for the greatest car of all time continues, Stuart Hall makes the case for the Rolls Royce Silver Cloud. The Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is the now sadly departed `Countdown' host and pun master extraordinaire Richard Whiteley.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: A Renault Megane has a test crash with a real person inside, and the Hummer H2 and Talon Riot Control Vehicle are put through their paces. With guest star Neil Morrissey.

programme.title: Motorhoming With Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and his wife Suki Webster are joining the masses on the A-roads, embracing Britain's booming motorhoming scene, seeking out the UK's most famous sights and hidden gems.

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins his journey in the south of Ireland, para-motoring with an Irish explorer. On the west coast he does spot of surfing, before climbing Croagh Patrick in honour of Ireland's patron saint. This leg of his journey ends in Malin Head, Ireland's most northerly point.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to the picturesque River Usk in Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. Their quarry is the wild brown trout. They discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob brave the wild lakes of Essex in search of the mighty carp. They have the latest high tech equipment and are fully prepared to stay all night if they have to, to catch the biggest fish they can.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon so they set off for The River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement for this special trip. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They discuss luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob has harboured a childhood dream to catch a salmon so they set off for The River Tay in Scotland full of hope and excitement for this special trip. A ghillie guides them up the intimidating river in a boat and they fish using various methods to catch the elusive fish. They discuss luck and fate and the part it has played in their lives.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fly to Enniskillen to fish for Pike. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced local Frenchman Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample the traditional Irish refreshments.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob fish for the graceful Grayling on the stunning River Ure in the heart of the Yorkshire Dales. They talk about their plans for retirement and Bob reveals his hopes and dreams for the future. Together they meet the surgeon who performed Bob's life-saving heart bypass and re-enact all the drama of the operating table, in a local pub.

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon begins his journey in the south of Ireland, para-motoring with an Irish explorer. On the west coast he does spot of surfing, before climbing Croagh Patrick in honour of Ireland's patron saint. This leg of his journey ends in Malin Head, Ireland's most northerly point.

programme.title: Ireland with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Starting at the spectacular Giant's Causeway in Northern Ireland, Simon travels down the east coast to the great and rapidly changing cities of Belfast and Dublin. He ends his journey in the stunning Wicklow Mountains.

programme.title: Borderforce: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: A wanted fugitive tries to escape as officers take her fingerprints, and a doctor entering America is caught smuggling in prescription medication worth thousands of dollars.

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Britain's most talented detectives tackle a dangerous arsonist in Bristol, a drug smuggling operation in Manchester, and a family committing shocking animal cruelty in Cheshire.

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Tinker is behaving very strangely - he abandons Beth at a bus stop, ruins a deal for Lovejoy when he fails to spot a false leg on an 18th-century piece of furniture, walks out of a pub leaving a pint of beer half drunk, and then disappears entirely leaving no clue as to his whereabouts.

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: An eccentric group of ex-police officers are brought out of retirement to investigate unsolved crimes. Lane's future at UCOS is threatened by a death in custody which emerges from his past. Meanwhile, the discovery of a mysterious gun in the Thames re-opens the unsolved 1998 murder of a playboy shipping heir and leads the team to the Rock of Gibraltar.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team unlock over three decades of secrets and lies on the Rock of Gibraltar and find the link which solves the murder of a playboy shipping heir from '98 and a young local boy from '82. With both cases solved, they return to England where Lane faces a disciplinary hearing, dragging up a death in custody from his past which threatens his career in UCOS.

programme.title: Call the Midwife

programme.title: Catherine Cookson Dramas

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Drama series set in a Cheshire village during the Second World War. The isolated community could not feel further away from the impending bloodshed and battlefields, yet it is not immune from the effects of war. Everyone must make sacrifices as war starts to have an impact on the village. As Pat's marriage deteriorates, Kate and Jack's relationship intensifies.

In a show of brotherly love, Del naturally offers to accompany Rodders, and so they bid farewell to Peckham and embark on a holiday they are likely never to forget.

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses...
programme.sub-title: Miami Twice: Oh, to Be in England
programme.desc: With Del handling the accommodation and transport for their week's holiday in America, it's not surprising that he and Rodney end up in a dodgy camper-van. Things get even worse when the van disappears while they are in a nightclub.

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses...
programme.desc: Del knows that the world is his oyster, if only he can discover the whereabouts of Freddie the Frog's legacy of gold bullion. In the meantime, he has to keep the money coming in by flogging infra-red massagers to punters in the market, with Uncle Albert demonstrating their fantastic healing properties.

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses...
programme.desc: The Trotters' finances hit an all-time low and wheeler-dealer Del tries to improve himself with a `new man' manual. Meanwhile, with Cassandra expecting, Rodders decides he needs another job.

programme.title: Only Fools and Horses...

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Alice solves the problem of the Christmas show by proposing a promenading Nativity, in which she and Hugo will play Mary and Joseph. However, when her waters break mid-performance, she has to check with Geraldine whether she has actually given birth to the baby Jesus.

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Bishop of Mulberry is to officiate at the christening of Alice and Hugo's baby. Geraldine and Owen are to be the godparents while Frank and Jim act as god-grandparents. When an amazed David finds that he and Geraldine are well matched, he asks her to marry him.

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The committee members agree to erect a statue in the green, but they cannot find a heroic figure from Dibley's past.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: When the vicar and Mrs Dingle decide to reopen the youth club, they enlist Terry's help to assemble a ping-pong table. The instructions should be idiot-proof, but whoever wrote them had not taken Terry into account.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: One-upmanship is the name of the game when Terry discovers that a junior member of the office staff has a satellite dish.

programme.title: Terry and June

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: After an attempted break-in, Terry makes his own burglar alarm.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: When the landlord of the local pub is advised to take a rest by his doctor, Terry and June offer to run the place in his absence. However, their good deed looks set to backfire when Sir Dennis threatens to sack anyone found guilty of moonlighting.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry loves to be the centre of attention, but when he discovers he bears an uncanny resemblance to a criminal, he wishes the spotlight would focus on someone else.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry experiences pangs of jealousy when June arranges to meet a childhood sweetheart. Misunderstandings and advice from Sir Dennis compound the situation.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry and June play the cow in the office pantomime but have difficulty getting out of their costume when the hall is evacuated because of a fire scare.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Newly promoted Sergeant Jo Masters and PC Leon Taylor are called to an aggravated burglary which has left the homeowner, John Baker, dead. As DS Max Carter and DC Grace Dasari investigate, they discover that Baker's wife Tanisha has been on a missing persons list for 20 years, and further questioning establishes that she ran away from home to marry John because her parents disapproved of the relationship.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When a letter bomb is delivered to the offices of a high profile company, the manager believes it to be a hoax, but Grace is concerned that it is the beginning of something sinister. The officers suspect that a regular courier is behind it - the man had been thrown out of the building a few days previously for harassing a PA.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sgt Stone and PC Leon Taylor find themselves involved in an armed siege after Alan Wilcock reports that his mentally unbalanced ex-wife Carly has locked herself in his house with their young son. When she discharges a firearm, CO19 are called in to diffuse the situation, and Sgt Jo Masters quickly establishes a rapport with the aggrieved woman through an internet link, but she becomes increasingly hysterical as time goes by.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sgt Callum Stone is on his way home from a night out when he sees couple Clarinda Blake and Tim Hardacre arguing in an alley. When Tim starts to hit Clarinda, Callum goes to her defence but gets a severe beating in return. Clarinda is traced and questioned but insists she was the victim of a random mugging. It emerges that Clarinda's teenage son Andy had been dealing weed for Tim and when Clarinda found out, she confronted him.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sgt Callum Stone and Sgt Jo Masters respond to a call to deal with a fracas outside a nightclub. Attending to the injured, Stone uses more force than necessary to stop a passer-by interfering and the incident is captured on a mobile phone and posted online. When the passer-by brings charges against Stone, new Sun Hill Commander Lisa Kennedy and Supt Jack Meadows worry about the media fallout and start an inquiry.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: When PC Nate Roberts and PC Benjamin Gayle are caught up in a shooting, Inspector Dale Smith and Sergeant Jo Masters are called to begin an investigation. An eye witness claims he saw a car speeding off, and the car's owner, Malcolm Christie, is brought in for questioning. However, when little forensic evidence is found on the car, the police are forced to let Christie go.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Sergeant Jo Masters and the team are called to a road traffic accident where a motorist has hit a youth who has since fled the scene. Then a passing cyclist discovers a teenage boy lying fatally injured in a nearby park. At first it seems the incidents are related - but are they?

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team race to save an injured woman trapped in a van crushed between two lorries.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Featuring a stand-off involving a man with a samurai sword in South Yorkshire.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: HART staff must treat one of their own after a freak accident involving a 4x4 leaves an off-duty medic with serious injuries.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team are called into action when an explosion demolishes a block of flats.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The response team race to the rescue of a worker trapped in a collapsed trench, and paramedic Kim must risk her own life to give him medical treatment 10ft underground.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A truck driver is trapped in his crushed cab after a motorway smash, while in Hull, firefighters handcuff a man to the rails of the Humber Bridge to stop him from jumping.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A farmer is trapped under his rolled quad; backyard DYI ends in a disaster; a teen experiences crippling abdominal pain; a stroke patient needs urgent help.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A crash on a high-powered motorbike leaves a man in critical condition; a five-year-old fights an asthma attack; a Waiheke woman suffers a scary cardiac event.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: Thomas goes down memory lane with Alison, however the truth is muddied when the other ghosts tell their version of events. Mike struggles with his own memories of Alison's past.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's a nervous Mike's first night alone in the house, when some uninvited guests set the ghosts on a mission to defend their treasured home.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: The big day has arrived at Button House, but some freak weather disrupts everyone's plans. Can Mike, Alison and the ghosts rally to pull off what they've been working so hard to achieve?

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nan meets her grandson's girlfriend; Lauren decides to get a tattoo of her boyfriend; new bride Victoria makes a wedding speech.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Elaine seeks love online and meets a promising man from Egypt, to whom she sends money, We meet Sam's parents, and Nan goes to a funeral.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren asks her teacher if he's gay, Nan and an old friend render `Bridge Over Troubled Water', and Kate asks Ellen to guess which is the worst swearword used in the office.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: PR-land quakes with anticipation as the time for dishing out awards approaches. Edina, having paid for them, naturally expects to win her fair share, but arch rival Claudia Bing has other ideas.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy moves in to live with Edina when Saffy moves out. However, it proves to be a turbulent arrangement, especially when they can't find the tin opener. Further trouble erupts when Lulu arrives at Edina's office to terminate her contract and sign with a high-powered American agency.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina has gone to a retreat to find herself. Saffy has moved out, and Patsy has gone to work for a magazine in New York. With Mother left alone in the house, will life ever be the same again?

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan take part in Michael's school fundraising auction. However, Ben quickly regrets making a pledge when he spies a former patient with severe halitosis bidding for a double session in his dentist's chair.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Susan has taken to asking an increasingly disturbed Michael for advice on make-up and clothes now that Janey has left for university. The arrival of Ben's cousin's daughter in the Harper household could provide a solution - but Ben is reluctant to give up the peace and quiet he has spent his entire married life longing for.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers star in a documentary about family life as part of Abi's latest college assignment. Susan is happy to present a facade of domestic bliss and dispense cookery tips along with relationship advice. Ben, however, is more concerned with catching a rogue rodent.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Ben and Susan look forward to a night out in a town where nobody knows their names when they visit daughter Janey, who is away at university. Booking into a hotel under the names of Johnny Studd and Delores de Large, the stage is set for a night full of surprises, of one kind or another.

programme.title: My Family
programme.sub-title: The Second Greatest Story Ever Told
programme.desc: Susan's feathers are ruffled when Nick lands the plum part of Jesus in an amateur theatrical production, leaving Susan to play the rather less demanding part of Shepherd Number Two. Ben, meanwhile, has an extremely painful toothache to contend with, and is wary of entrusting his dental needs to any other dentist.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: After Susan finds marijuana in Michael's room, which he got from his friend Bex, she decides to teach him a lesson but finds herself scrambling after she `confiscates' the evidence.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: Tipping Point

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A five-year-old Great Dane is rushed to Fitzpatrick Referrals after losing the use of her back legs. Michelle and her two young children bring their spaniel to the practice after it's hit by a car.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Newfoundland called Billy clocks up thousands of miles travelling back and forth from his home in Aberdeen for treatment on his legs. Tiny Yorkshire terrier Barney has a deformity in his neck which is compressing his spinal cord.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Ruth and her husband Chris have spent their lives saving stray animals. They have a three-legged cat called Jersey, who has recently become lame on her single back leg, and Professor Fitzpatrick has to decide if a hip replacement is viable.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Fitzpatrick Referrals is used to dealing with emergencies, but when a young cockapoo called Orla gets hit by a lawn mower, Professor Noel Fitzpatrick fears the worst. Noel's challenge is to save as much of the leg as possible.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick helps Labrador Hetty, who has become lame on both her front legs, and receives a visit from Ella and Rob, who bring their Rhodesian Ridgeback to see if surgery can save his leg from a cancerous tumour.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews attend a playground head injury, a patient with multiple fainting episodes, a man with suspected DVT, and visit a convent to treat a nun with chest pain.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Oxford, crews help a child with signs of meningitis and take an injured cyclist to hospital. In Portsmouth, they help a man who's fallen and a patient with Crohn's Disease.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: An eight-year-old is having an operation to straighten his squint, a man needs an ultrasound to confirm whether he has cancer and a new mum is having her wisdom teeth removed.

programme.title: Inside the Operating Theatre
programme.desc: A 15-year-old has four baby teeth that have not fallen out and need removing, while a woman needs an operation to stop her persistent nose bleeds.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nan meets her grandson's girlfriend; Lauren decides to get a tattoo of her boyfriend; new bride Victoria makes a wedding speech.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Elaine seeks love online and meets a promising man from Egypt, to whom she sends money, We meet Sam's parents, and Nan goes to a funeral.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Lauren asks her teacher if he's gay, Nan and an old friend render `Bridge Over Troubled Water', and Kate asks Ellen to guess which is the worst swearword used in the office.

programme.title: Who Killed Jenni Rivera?
programme.desc: Rocked by threats and deceptions within her inner circle, Jenni decides to make significant changes in her life. Two people aboard the plane allegedly have ties with the cartel.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy has had a Parralox injection to freeze her wrinkles. Saffy is trying to get a job with New Labour, and Edina, who has now added a TV company to her business portfolio, has got herself an appearance booked on This Morning.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Edina returns from a Marilyn Manson concert, and spies a handsome young man working up a sweat in her garden the following morning. Patsy immediately recognises him as the younger brother of a well-known aristocrat.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: A photoshoot in Paris gives Patsy the chance to relive her modelling days, but the event is not quite the glamorous trip down memory lane she had expected and she struggles to cope with fresh-faced young models.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy visits Llandudno to help get this Queen of Victorian resorts up to scratch in time for the summer season. The great British seaside holiday was a largely Victorian invention.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy's project here is the first piston engine ever built. The Newcomen Beam Engine was the first practical device to harness the power of steam and kick-started the Industrial Revolution by allowing coal mining to operate on an industrial scale.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory hunt for neglected cars they can fix and flip for a profit. Any losses incurred by their flipping come out of their own pockets.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The guys take on a major challenge when they spot an abandoned Reliant Kitten. One engineer has already given up. Are Will and Gus about to do the same?

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: Stories of the D-Day invasion are recounted by everyone from a British sailor who laid underwater charges to blow up German defences to Canadians, Americans, French resistance fighters and Nazi defenders.

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: Interviews are combined with newly discovered footage and dramatic reconstructions to tell the story of D-Day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce is in Normandy for a special edition of the roadshow to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings in June 1944. Filmed in Normandy and at the D-Day Museum in Portsmouth, the team hear eyewitness accounts from the time.

programme.title: World War 2 from Above
programme.desc: Using drone radar, experts uncover a WWII German trench system revealing a daring allied paratrooper raid to capture it. Also, underwater drone pilots try to identify a lost US vessel.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A cherished wartime iron workhorse needs to be hauled 160 miles from the Churnet Valley Railway in Staffordshire to a winter extravaganza in Oxfordshire.

programme.title: Churchill's Toyshop
programme.desc: MD1, a group of scientists and engineers brought together by Churchill, came up with a range of ingenious weapons and inventions to combat the Nazi war machine.

programme.title: Guy Martin Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Guy Martin trains as a Battle of Britain fighter pilot and prepares to face a Messerschmitt in a dogfight. But his toughest test yet starts with a near disaster on take-off!

programme.title: Guy Martin Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Guy's bid to be a fighter pilot ramps up at aerial combat school, flying upside down and dogfights at 200mph. Finally, he tackles a Messerschmitt 109 over Kent. Will he win the day?

programme.title: Guy Martin's D-Day Landing
programme.desc: To mark D-Day's 75th anniversary, Guy Martin restores a Dakota plane, undergoes brutal training with the Red Devils parachute display team, and parachutes into the Normandy countryside.

programme.title: Robson Green: World's Most Amazing Walks
programme.desc: Robson Green walks along the north coast of Normandy, France, tracing the footsteps of brave soldiers of the D-Day Landings.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The guys buy a confusing 1984 French Matra Murena and send it to Citroën specialists. Next, they take on a Bond classic - no, not that one - a 1977 Arctic Wetbike!

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie sets off from Runcorn on the Bridgewater Canal, but a series of unfortunate mishaps soon tests his patience - from lost camera footage to closed canals.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy visits Llandudno to help get this Queen of Victorian resorts up to scratch in time for the summer season. The great British seaside holiday was a largely Victorian invention.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy's project here is the first piston engine ever built. The Newcomen Beam Engine was the first practical device to harness the power of steam and kick-started the Industrial Revolution by allowing coal mining to operate on an industrial scale.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Will Trickett and Gus Gregory hunt for neglected cars they can fix and flip for a profit. Any losses incurred by their flipping come out of their own pockets.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The guys take on a major challenge when they spot an abandoned Reliant Kitten. One engineer has already given up. Are Will and Gus about to do the same?

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: Stories of the D-Day invasion are recounted by everyone from a British sailor who laid underwater charges to blow up German defences to Canadians, Americans, French resistance fighters and Nazi defenders.

programme.title: D-Day: Last Words
programme.desc: Interviews are combined with newly discovered footage and dramatic reconstructions to tell the story of D-Day.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce is in Normandy for a special edition of the roadshow to commemorate the 80th anniversary of the D-Day landings in June 1944. Filmed in Normandy and at the D-Day Museum in Portsmouth, the team hear eyewitness accounts from the time.

programme.title: World War 2 from Above
programme.desc: Using drone radar, experts uncover a WWII German trench system revealing a daring allied paratrooper raid to capture it. Also, underwater drone pilots try to identify a lost US vessel.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A cherished wartime iron workhorse needs to be hauled 160 miles from the Churnet Valley Railway in Staffordshire to a winter extravaganza in Oxfordshire.

programme.title: Churchill's Toyshop
programme.desc: MD1, a group of scientists and engineers brought together by Churchill, came up with a range of ingenious weapons and inventions to combat the Nazi war machine.

programme.title: Guy Martin Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Guy Martin trains as a Battle of Britain fighter pilot and prepares to face a Messerschmitt in a dogfight. But his toughest test yet starts with a near disaster on take-off!

programme.title: Guy Martin Battle of Britain
programme.desc: Guy's bid to be a fighter pilot ramps up at aerial combat school, flying upside down and dogfights at 200mph. Finally, he tackles a Messerschmitt 109 over Kent. Will he win the day?

programme.title: Guy Martin's D-Day Landing
programme.desc: To mark D-Day's 75th anniversary, Guy Martin restores a Dakota plane, undergoes brutal training with the Red Devils parachute display team, and parachutes into the Normandy countryside.

programme.title: Robson Green: World's Most Amazing Walks
programme.desc: Robson Green walks along the north coast of Normandy, France, tracing the footsteps of brave soldiers of the D-Day Landings.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy helps the £500,000 restoration of the world's oldest surviving Brixham sailing trawler, the boat that launched the modern fishing industry and transformed the way a nation ate. He experiences deep sea trawling, said to be the most dangerous job of the 19th century, for himself, and discovers how Britain acquired its taste for fish and chips.

programme.title: How Britain Worked
programme.desc: Guy helps to get the Birmingham Botanical Gardens back into shape, and tries to learn one of the Industrial Revolution's most skilful and dangerous jobs: blowing glass for the gardens' enormous greenhouses. He has a go at rebuilding the very first lawnmower: another great British invention. Guy also uses a little-known 19th-century technique to make his own rock.

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On an epic railway journey from Lucknow in Uttar Pradesh to Kolkata in West Bengal, Michael Portillo uses his Bradshaw's 1913 Handbook of Indian, Foreign and Colonial Travel, published when the British Raj was fifty-five years old, to chart a course through India's history from the days of The East India Company to the dawn of independence.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning in the Silver State of Nevada, Michael takes to the skies over the dramatic Sierra Nevada mountain range. At Lake Tahoe, he hears of the first European explorer, known as `The Pathfinder', whose discovery opened up the west for settlement. Travelling on the historic Virginia and Truckee heritage line, Michael investigates the vast deposits of silver and gold ore on which Virginia City was built.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chinatown to hear of the huge contribution Chinese immigrants made to the economy, working as miners and building the transcontinental railroad. He discovers an entrepreneurial community who overcame discrimination laid down by the law, and finds that fortune favours the brave with iconic Chinese treats created in America.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Antarctica, a land of survivors enduring the most hostile conditions on Earth. Ninety eight per cent of the mainland is covered by ice on which virtually nothing can live. Even the sea freezes over, but the Weddell seal manages to survive here by keeping its breathing holes open by using its teeth to grind away the ice.

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: Asia is the largest and most extreme continent on our planet, stretching from the Arctic Circle in the north to the tropical forests on the equator. The animals here face the hottest deserts, tallest jungles and highest mountains found anywhere on Earth. But the continent has not always looked like this. These extreme worlds were created when India collided with the rest of Asia 30 million years ago, shaping the continent as we know it today.
